top_signals.title: Why the Strait of Hormuz Is at the Heart of the US-Iran Stalemate
top_signals.summary: No region of the world produces more oil and gas than the countries straddling the Persian Gulf. Most of this energy can only be exported aboard tankers that cross the Strait of Hormuz — a waterway that’s effectively been blocked for more than three months.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Pakistan’s Fuel Sales Slump as War Boosts Prices and Hits Demand
top_signals.summary: Fuel sales in Pakistan have slumped as costlier prices triggered by the Iran war force consumers to cut back, according to AKD Securities Ltd.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Nippon Paint and Sherwin-Williams End Pursuit of Akzo Nobel
top_signals.summary: Nippon Paint Holdings Co. and Sherwin-Williams Co. ended their effort to buy Akzo Nobel NV, leaving the Dutch paintmaker to proceed with its planned merger with US firm Axalta Coating Systems Ltd.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: What Henry Nowak’s murder does — and does not — tell us about policing
top_signals.summary: Kemi Badenoch says his killing needs to be seen as a ‘Stephen Lawrence moment’ but the failures in both cases differ
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Alphabet's plan to sell $80 billion in stock to fund its AI buildout isn't all bad
top_signals.summary: It's the latest step in an aggressive effort by big tech companies to secure future funding for AI infrastructure.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
